content: Anticoagulation indications and dosing: Atrial fibrillation (CHA2DS2-VASc ≥2 men/≥3 women): apixaban 5mg BID (reduce to 2.5mg BID if ≥2 of age ≥80, weight ≤60kg, creatinine ≥1.5), rivaroxaban 20mg daily with evening meal (15mg if CrCl 30-50), dabigatran 150mg BID (75mg if CrCl 15-30), edoxaban 60mg daily (30mg if CrCl 15-50, weight ≤60kg, or P-gp inhibitor). VTE treatment: acute phase (first 5-21 days) requires therapeutic anticoagulation; DOACs have loading strategies (rivaroxaban 15mg BID x21 days then 20mg; apixaban 10mg BID x7 days then 5mg); warfarin requires bridging with LMWH until INR therapeutic. Extended treatment: indefinite for unprovoked proximal DVT/PE or ongoing risk factors; 3 months for provoked VTE with resolved risk factor. Cancer-associated thrombosis: DOACs now preferred over LMWH (SELECT-D, CARAVAGGIO trials) except GI/GU cancers with high bleeding risk. Orthopedic prophylaxis: 10-35 days post-surgery depending on procedure.
